# zod

<!-- archie:ai-start -->

> Auto-generated Zod validation schemas for all OpenMeter API query parameters and request bodies, produced by orval from the OpenAPI spec. Exports typed Zod objects plus per-operation constant defaults/regex patterns — never hand-edit.

## Patterns

**Generated by orval — do not edit** — index.ts carries the 'Generated by orval v8.7.0 / Do not edit manually.' header. All schemas are regenerated from the OpenAPI spec; manual edits are overwritten on the next make gen-api. (`/** Generated by orval v8.7.0 🍺 Do not edit manually. */`)
**Constants exported before each schema** — Each operation exports named constants for defaults, min/max, and RegExp patterns before its zod.object() (e.g. listAddonsQueryPageDefault = 1, listAddonsQueryKeyItemRegExp), referenced inside the schema. (`export const listAddonsQueryPageSizeMax = 1000
export const ListAddonsQueryParams = zod.object({ pageSize: zod.coerce.number().min(1).max(listAddonsQueryPageSizeMax)... })`)
**zod.coerce for all scalar fields** — Query parameters use zod.coerce.string()/number()/boolean() to handle URL string coercion, matching how form-encoded query strings arrive. (`includeDeleted: zod.coerce.boolean().default(listAddonsQueryIncludeDeletedDefault)`)
**Naming convention per operation** — Schema objects are named <OperationPascalCase>QueryParams or <OperationPascalCase>Body (e.g. ListAddonsQueryParams, CreateAddonBody). (`export const ListAddonsQueryParams = zod.object({ ... }); export const CreateAddonBody = zod.object({ ... })`)

## Key Files

| File | Role | Watch For |
|------|------|-----------|
| `index.ts` | Single generated file exporting all Zod schemas for query-param and body validation, plus the per-operation constants (defaults, regexps, min/max). | DO NOT EDIT — regenerated by orval on make gen-api. Import these schemas for server-side validation of incoming params. |

## Anti-Patterns

- Manually editing index.ts — it is regenerated by orval from the OpenAPI spec on make gen-api.
- Redefining Zod schemas locally when the generated schemas already cover the operation.
- Using the raw generated constants (defaults/regexps) in application logic — they are schema implementation details subject to regeneration.

## Decisions

- **Zod schemas are generated by orval from the same OpenAPI spec that generates the TypeScript types.** — Keeps runtime validation (Zod) and compile-time types (TypeScript) in sync with the TypeSpec source of truth — a single make gen-api updates both.

## Example: Using a generated query-param schema for server-side validation

```
import { ListAddonsQueryParams } from '@openmeter/sdk/zod'
const parsed = ListAddonsQueryParams.parse(req.query)
```

<!-- archie:ai-end -->
